reasoning-chain-of-thought.claim_text: Explicit step-by-step reasoning prompts (Chain-of-Thought, Tree-of-Thought, Graph-of-Thought, Self-Consistency, Plan-and-Solve) materially raise LLM performance on multi-step problems and form the prompting backbone of every modern agent.

candidates.paper_title: Large language models encode clinical knowledge

useful_outcomes.rationale: Med-PaLM uses instruction prompt tuning, demonstrates CoT-style reasoning gains on MedQA (67.6%) but CoT not central contribution.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Clinical QA results actionable; CoT mechanism only one of several prompting techniques evaluated.

plausible.rationale: Nature-published, large evaluation; reasoning improvements with scale plausibly shown via human eval.

scalable.rationale: Med-PaLM scales prompting but domain-specific to medicine, not general CoT framework.

how_to_validate.rationale: MedQA, MedMCQA benchmarks provide reproducible validation; human eval rubrics included.

candidates.paper_title: Sparks of Artificial General Intelligence: Early experiments with GPT-4

useful_outcomes.rationale: Documents GPT-4 multi-step reasoning across domains but is a broad capability survey, not a CoT method paper.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Qualitative reasoning case studies inform agent design but no concrete benchmark numbers for CoT variants.

plausible.rationale: Microsoft Research extensive probing of GPT-4; widely cited (1542) reasoning observations.

scalable.rationale: Observations apply to frontier LLMs generally; not a portable technique.

how_to_validate.rationale: Largely anecdotal/qualitative probes, limited reproducible benchmark protocols.

candidates.paper_title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces

useful_outcomes.rationale: Mamba is an architecture (selective SSM) for long sequences; not about CoT/ToT/GoT prompting.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Does not provide prompting recipes for multi-step reasoning.

plausible.rationale: Strong architecture work but tangential to claim about explicit reasoning prompts.

scalable.rationale: Linear-time scaling is real but unrelated to reasoning prompts.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reasoning-prompt-specific validation absent; benchmarks are language modeling.

candidates.paper_title: A Prompt Pattern Catalog to Enhance Prompt Engineering with ChatGPT

useful_outcomes.rationale: Catalog includes step-by-step decomposition patterns; broader prompt-engineering scope than CoT alone.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Practitioner-ready patterns directly usable for designing agent prompts.

plausible.rationale: White Vanderbilt prompt-pattern paper; well-structured but more taxonomy than empirical proof.

scalable.rationale: Patterns are model-agnostic and portable across LLMs.

how_to_validate.rationale: Limited quantitative benchmarks; mostly illustrative examples.

candidates.paper_title: Toward expert-level medical question answering with large language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Med-PaLM 2 uses ensemble refinement / self-consistency CoT for MedQA; demonstrates reasoning gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Medical-domain CoT pipeline; transferable ensemble-refinement idea to other agents.

plausible.rationale: Nature Medicine evaluation with clinician panels and multiple datasets.

scalable.rationale: Ensemble refinement is compute-heavy; principles generalize.

how_to_validate.rationale: MedQA, MedMCQA, PubMedQA benchmarks plus human rubric provide rigorous validation.

candidates.paper_title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey

useful_outcomes.rationale: RAG survey discusses chain-of-thought retrieval interplay but core topic is retrieval, not CoT.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Indirect: RAG often combined with CoT, but paper focuses on retrieval pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Survey citing canonical RAG/CoT works; reasoning role addressed only briefly.

scalable.rationale: Retrieval techniques scale; relation to claim mainly via combined RAG+CoT systems.

how_to_validate.rationale: Survey enumerates benchmarks but does not validate CoT prompting specifically.

candidates.paper_title: A Multitask, Multilingual, Multimodal Evaluation of ChatGPT on Reasoning, Hallucination, and Interactivity

useful_outcomes.rationale: Evaluates ChatGPT on multi-step reasoning tasks across 23 datasets; informs where CoT helps.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Benchmark insights directly usable for agent designers; identifies CoT failure modes.

plausible.rationale: Bang et al. multitask eval with extensive reasoning benchmarks; well-cited (606).

scalable.rationale: Findings span many languages/modalities but ChatGPT-specific.

how_to_validate.rationale: Public benchmarks (MMLU, BBH, etc.) with documented zero/few-shot CoT protocols.

candidates.paper_title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: ToT directly named in claim; raises Game-of-24 from 4% (CoT) to 74% with GPT-4.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Concrete search-over-thoughts algorithm immediately usable in agents.

plausible.rationale: Yao et al. landmark with explicit benchmarks (Game-of-24, creative writing, crosswords).

scalable.rationale: Search adds inference cost but generalizes across tasks; framework portable.

how_to_validate.rationale: Open code, three distinct task suites with quantitative comparisons to CoT/self-consistency.

candidates.paper_title: Detecting hallucinations in large language models using semantic entropy

useful_outcomes.rationale: Semantic-entropy hallucination detection complements CoT but is not a reasoning prompt method.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful for agent reliability layer; tangential to step-by-step reasoning prompts.

plausible.rationale: Nature paper with rigorous entropy estimator and benchmark sweeps.

scalable.rationale: Method generalizes across models but not a CoT scaling lever.

how_to_validate.rationale: TriviaQA/NQ-style benchmarks; reproducible but off-topic for the claim.

candidates.paper_title: G-Eval: NLG Evaluation using Gpt-4 with Better Human Alignment

useful_outcomes.rationale: G-Eval uses CoT-based GPT-4 evaluation; demonstrates CoT improves NLG-eval alignment with humans.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides usable CoT eval prompts (SummEval, Topical-Chat) for agent eval pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Liu et al. show Spearman correlation gains using CoT auto-eval at scale.

scalable.rationale: Approach transfers to other NLG tasks though cost depends on GPT-4 calls.

how_to_validate.rationale: SummEval and Topical-Chat human correlations; reproducible protocol.

reasoning-tool-use.claim_text: Letting an LLM emit structured tool calls (ReAct, Toolformer, function-calling APIs, HuggingGPT) turns it from a text generator into an agent that can search, compute, query APIs, and execute code — closing the gap between knowledge and action.

candidates.paper_title: Segment anything in medical images

useful_outcomes.rationale: MedSAM is a segmentation foundation model, not an LLM tool-calling system.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Could be invoked as a tool by an LLM agent, but paper does not demonstrate that.

plausible.rationale: High-quality vision work; weak fit to tool-use-by-LLM claim.

scalable.rationale: Model itself scales but not a tool-orchestration framework.

how_to_validate.rationale: No tool-use protocol or benchmark provided.

candidates.paper_title: Foundation models for generalist medical artificial intelligence

useful_outcomes.rationale: GMAI vision paper discusses tool/modality combination but does not implement structured tool calls.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Position piece; little immediate implementation guidance for tool-calling agents.

plausible.rationale: Influential Nature perspective on multi-modal medical AI.

scalable.rationale: Articulates scalable vision but not concrete tool-use methodology.

how_to_validate.rationale: Perspective with no empirical tool-call evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: CheckM2: a rapid, scalable and accurate tool for assessing microbial genome quality using machine learning

useful_outcomes.rationale: Bioinformatics ML tool unrelated to LLM tool calling.

immediate_benefit.rationale: No agent/tool-call relevance.

plausible.rationale: High-quality but off-topic for claim.

scalable.rationale: Could be wrapped as a callable tool but paper does not.

how_to_validate.rationale: No tool-use validation.

candidates.paper_title: Computational approaches streamlining drug discovery

useful_outcomes.rationale: Drug-discovery computational review; not LLM tool-calling.

immediate_benefit.rationale: No direct tool-use methodology for agents.

plausible.rationale: Review piece; tangential to claim.

scalable.rationale: Methods scalable but unrelated framing.

how_to_validate.rationale: No agent-tool benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: Autonomous chemical research with large language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Coscientist couples GPT-4 with web search, Python, and robotic liquid handlers to run Pd reactions.

immediate_benefit.rationale: End-to-end LLM tool-calling agent executing real wet-lab experiments.

plausible.rationale: Nature paper with multiple validated tool-using experiments.

scalable.rationale: Modular tool-use design generalizes to other domains and instruments.

how_to_validate.rationale: Specific case studies (Suzuki/Sonogashira) reproducible by other chemistry agents.

candidates.paper_title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey

useful_outcomes.rationale: Retrieval is a canonical tool; survey covers tool-augmented LLMs and retrieval-as-tool patterns.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful taxonomy for agent designers integrating retrieval tools.

plausible.rationale: Comprehensive RAG survey; well cited (638).

scalable.rationale: Patterns scale across retrieval backends and tool wrappers.

how_to_validate.rationale: Lists benchmarks but does not run new tool-use experiments.

candidates.paper_title: GPTs are GPTs: An Early Look at the Labor Market Impact Potential of Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Labor-market study; LLM-powered software framing but not a tool-use method.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Economic analysis offers no implementation for tool-calling agents.

plausible.rationale: OpenAI/UPenn economic paper; off-topic for claim.

scalable.rationale: Findings broad but not method-scalable.

how_to_validate.rationale: No tool-use benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: Augmenting large language models with chemistry tools

useful_outcomes.rationale: ChemCrow integrates 18 expert chemistry tools (RDKit, retrosynthesis, etc.) into GPT-4 agent.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Reference design for tool-calling LLM agents; outperforms GPT-4 baselines on chemistry tasks.

plausible.rationale: Nature MI paper with expert evaluation of synthesis planning.

scalable.rationale: Pattern extends to any domain with API/CLI tools.

how_to_validate.rationale: Released code and benchmark tasks; expert chemist comparisons documented.

candidates.paper_title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools

useful_outcomes.rationale: Toolformer directly named in claim: self-supervised API-call training for calculator, search, QA, translate.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Concrete method (self-generated API-call data) reusable in any LLM tool-use stack.

plausible.rationale: Schick et al. Meta AI landmark; clear empirical gains over GPT-3 baseline on zero-shot tasks.

scalable.rationale: Self-supervised pipeline scales but per-tool annotation curated.

how_to_validate.rationale: LAMA, SQuAD, ASDiv, GSM8K, WebQuestions zero-shot benchmarks reported.

candidates.paper_title: HuggingGPT: Solving AI Tasks with ChatGPT and its Friends in Hugging Face

useful_outcomes.rationale: HuggingGPT named in claim: ChatGPT orchestrates HF models for vision/audio/text task pipelines.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Plan-Select-Execute-Generate pattern reusable for any tool-rich LLM agent.

plausible.rationale: Shen et al. extensive multi-modal demos; method clearly described.

scalable.rationale: Scales with HF model hub; coordination overhead grows with tool count.

how_to_validate.rationale: Open code, multi-domain task benchmarks (image, speech, NLP).

candidates.paper_title: Gorilla: Large Language Model Connected with Massive APIs

useful_outcomes.rationale: Gorilla fine-tunes LLaMA to emit correct API calls over 1,645 ML APIs (HF, TF Hub, TorchHub).

immediate_benefit.rationale: Direct recipe for retrieval-aware API-call fine-tuning in agents.

plausible.rationale: Patil et al. demonstrate >20pt accuracy gains over GPT-4 on API benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: Retrieval-aware training handles changing API docs at scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: APIBench released with hallucination/accuracy metrics.

candidates.paper_title: ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs

useful_outcomes.rationale: ToolLLM builds ToolBench across 16k RapidAPI APIs and trains ToolLLaMA matching ChatGPT tool use.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open dataset and training recipe directly usable for tool-using open-source agents.

plausible.rationale: Qin et al. landmark large-scale tool dataset; methodology well documented.

scalable.rationale: Scales to thousands of real APIs with DFSDT search.

how_to_validate.rationale: ToolEval benchmark with pass-rate and win-rate metrics released.

candidates.paper_title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Voyager uses GPT-4 to write executable Mineflayer code as tools, builds skill library in Minecraft.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code-as-tool plus skill-library pattern directly applicable to other embodied agents.

plausible.rationale: Wang et al. show 3.3x more unique items, faster tech-tree progress vs ReAct/AutoGPT baselines.

scalable.rationale: Skill library accumulates open-endedly; transferable across worlds.

how_to_validate.rationale: MineDojo/Minecraft benchmarks with quantitative progression metrics.

reasoning-memory-and-reflection.claim_text: Reflection / self-refinement loops (Reflexion, Self-Refine, Self-Discover) and persistent memory architectures (MemGPT, Memory-Augmented Transformers) let agents learn from their own mistakes within and across episodes — the bridge from stateless prompting to long-horizon autonomy.

candidates.paper_title: Large language models encode clinical knowledge

useful_outcomes.rationale: Med-PaLM uses instruction prompt tuning but not reflection/memory loops as defined in claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Limited reflection mechanism; clinical-domain focus.

plausible.rationale: Strong Nature paper but tangential to memory/reflection.

scalable.rationale: Domain-specific tuning, not a memory architecture.

how_to_validate.rationale: MedQA validation present but not reflection-specific.

candidates.paper_title: Sparks of Artificial General Intelligence: Early experiments with GPT-4

useful_outcomes.rationale: Discusses GPT-4 self-correction qualitatively; not a memory/reflection method paper.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Observations seed reflection ideas but no concrete loop algorithm.

plausible.rationale: Highly cited probing study; reflection mentions are anecdotal.

scalable.rationale: Generic observations, not architecture-specific.

how_to_validate.rationale: No reflection benchmark protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey

useful_outcomes.rationale: RAG is often used as external long-term memory; survey covers memory-augmented retrieval.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Retrieval-as-memory patterns directly usable in agent design.

plausible.rationale: Comprehensive survey but reflection loops not core.

scalable.rationale: Retrieval memory scales with vector stores.

how_to_validate.rationale: Many benchmarks listed; no reflection-specific eval.

candidates.paper_title: Augmenting large language models with chemistry tools

useful_outcomes.rationale: ChemCrow uses ReAct-style reflection over tool outputs; not its primary contribution.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Demonstrates reflection inside a tool-using agent in chemistry.

plausible.rationale: Nature MI; reflection loop documented in agent architecture.

scalable.rationale: Pattern transferable but coupled to ChemCrow tool set.

how_to_validate.rationale: Chemistry case studies, not memory benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reflexion named in claim: verbal RL self-reflection lifts HumanEval pass@1 to 91% with GPT-4.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Generic verbal feedback loop directly droppable into any LLM agent.

plausible.rationale: Shinn et al. canonical reflection work with strong reproducible numbers.

scalable.rationale: Scales across tasks (HotPotQA, ALFWorld, HumanEval) with low overhead.

how_to_validate.rationale: Three benchmarks released with code; clear ablation against baselines.

candidates.paper_title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback

useful_outcomes.rationale: Self-Refine named in claim: iterative self-feedback gains ~20% across 7 tasks with GPT-3.5/4.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Prompt-only refinement loop trivially integrable into agents.

plausible.rationale: Madaan et al. NeurIPS-quality work with multi-task validation.

scalable.rationale: Inference cost grows with iterations; otherwise broadly applicable.

how_to_validate.rationale: Seven tasks (math, code, dialogue, etc.) with public prompts and metrics.

candidates.paper_title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems

useful_outcomes.rationale: MemGPT named in claim: OS-style virtual context management for unbounded conversations/documents.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Reference architecture for persistent memory in agents; open implementation.

plausible.rationale: Packer et al. show gains on document analysis and multi-session chat over long contexts.

scalable.rationale: Paged memory scales to arbitrary context lengths.

how_to_validate.rationale: Document QA and conversation benchmarks released with code.

candidates.paper_title: Self-Discover: Large Language Models Self-Compose Reasoning Structures

useful_outcomes.rationale: Self-Discover named in claim: composes atomic reasoning modules; 32% gain on BBH with GPT-4.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Two-stage discovery prompt directly reusable; low overhead vs CoT-SC.

plausible.rationale: Zhou et al. with strong BBH/MATH/T4D improvements; lower citation count reflects recency.

scalable.rationale: Discovered structures reusable per task class; scales across models.

how_to_validate.rationale: BBH, MATH, Thinking-for-Doing benchmarks with comparison to CoT/PS.

candidates.paper_title: Plan-and-Solve Prompting: Improving Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought Reasoning by Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Plan-and-Solve is decomposition prompting; mild reflection via plan critique, not memory.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful prompt template; not a reflection loop or memory architecture.

plausible.rationale: Wang et al. ACL paper with multi-dataset arithmetic/commonsense gains.

scalable.rationale: Prompt-only and broadly portable.

how_to_validate.rationale: GSM8K, AQuA, SVAMP, ARC benchmarks reproducible.

candidates.paper_title: Graph of Thoughts: Solving Elaborate Problems with Large Language Models
candidates.paper_journal: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

useful_outcomes.rationale: GoT enables thought aggregation/refinement; has reflection flavor but core is reasoning topology.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Framework usable in agents that revisit thoughts but not a memory architecture.

plausible.rationale: Besta et al. AAAI 2024 with sorting/keyword counting gains over ToT.

scalable.rationale: Graph search generalizes across tasks; cost manageable.

how_to_validate.rationale: Open framework, quantitative comparisons to CoT/ToT released.
